problem.title: Moving Average from Data Stream
problem.description: Given a stream of integers and a window size, calculate the moving average of all integers in the sliding window. Implement the MovingAverage class with a constructor that takes the window size and a next(val) method that returns the moving average of the last 'size' values.
assumptions: Window contains at most 'size' most recent values.
next(val) is called for each new data point.
Return the average as a double.

example.explanation: Window size 3: [1]=1.0, [1,10]=5.5, [1,10,3]=4.67, [10,3,5]=6.0. Use a circular buffer (fixed array + head pointer) to avoid shifting elements — O(1) per insertion.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Circular Buffer
solutions.description: Use a fixed-size array as a circular buffer. Keep a running sum and a count of elements added. On each next() call, subtract the element being evicted (the oldest in the window), add the new element, and update the running sum. Return sum / min(count, size). The circular index is computed via modulo so no shifting is needed.
